==Super League== {{main|2005 Super League season}} Warrington's 2005 season was marked by the landmark short-term signing on 31 July of [[Newcastle Knights]] [[Rugby league positions#Scrum-half / half-back|scrum half]] [[Andrew Johns]], considered at the time to be the greatest rugby league player in the world, for the last two rounds of the season and for any forthcoming play-off fixture.{{cite news |title=Andrew Johns signing confirmed |url=https://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/news/5329316.andrew-johns-signing-confirmed/ |access-date=11 September 2025 |work=Warrington Guardian |date=3 August 2005}}{{cite news |title=Johns 'pumped' to get Wolves deal |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/sport1/hi/rugby_league/4729849.stm |access-date=11 September 2025 |publisher=BBC Sport |date=31 July 2005}} Johns, who made his Super League debut against the [[Leeds Rhinos]] on 12 September at home to a sold-out [[Halliwell Jones Stadium]], was signed by Warrington with the intention for the club to make it through to the [[2005 Super League Grand Final|Super League Grand Final]] for the first time in the club's history.{{cite news |title=Grand Final hopes ignite |url=https://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/news/5329396.grand-final-hopes-ignite/ |access-date=11 September 2025 |work=Warrington Guardian |date=4 August 2005}} Warrington ultimately lost at home to [[Hull F.C.]] in the elimination play-offs, triggering Johns' return to Australia.{{cite news |title=Warrington Wolves 6 Hull FC 40 |url=https://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/news/5331957.warrington-wolves-6-hull-fc-40/ |access-date=11 September 2025 |work=Warrington Guardian |date=26 September 2005}} ===Fixtures=== Warrington Wolves' 2005 Super League fixtures were released on 2 December 2004, with seven being televised live on [[Sky Sports]]:{{cite news |title=Start saving petrol money
